name: chat-service
description: Consumer back-end for chat.privasys.org. Owns per-user MCP tool state, fleet-governed tool resolution, and the signed tool-grant the chat front-end forwards to the confidential-ai enclave. PostgreSQL runs on the per-app sealed /data volume, so user state is encrypted at rest under a key only the app owner controls. This service is a REST API, not an MCP server, so it advertises no tools.

configure.name: configure
configure.title: Management service
configure.description: Point this back-end at the management-service control plane for its environment. It reads the fleet tool policy and validates enclave apps from there. It defaults to production, so this is only needed on dev/test.

mgmt_base_url.description: Management-service base URL for this environment.

x-privasys.label: Management-service base URL
x-privasys.details: chat-service reads the fleet tool_policy and validates enclave apps from this control plane. Leave unset to use the production default (https://api.developer.privasys.org); set the dev/test URL for those environments. Persisted to the sealed /data volume, so it survives restarts.

store.tagline: Consumer back-end for chat.privasys.org: per-user tools, fleet-governed tool resolution, and signed tool-grants.
store.keywords: chat, mcp, tools, tool grant, jwks, backend, confidential computing, attested, enclave, postgres
store.description:
chat-service is the consumer back-end behind chat.privasys.org. It owns each user's MCP tool list, resolves which tools a fleet permits, and mints the short-lived, signed tool-grant that the chat front-end forwards to the confidential-AI enclave.

All user state lives in PostgreSQL on the per-app sealed /data volume, encrypted at rest under a key only the app owner controls: the host, the operator, and Privasys never see the plaintext. The grant-signing key is generated and sealed on that same volume, so the published JWKS is stable across restarts and owner-approved upgrades, and the enclave can verify every grant against source-built, attested code.

This is a REST API, not an MCP server, so it advertises no tools of its own.
